narrative.headline: What 2014 Honda Cr-V owners report most
narrative.summary: Engine and powertrain complaints dominate owner reports, with many describing loss of traction and the vehicle sliding on slippery surfaces. Electrical system issues — particularly a tire pressure warning light that stays on from the moment of purchase and cannot be reset — account for a substantial share of complaints. Tire-related reports echo the sensor warning theme, with the indicator illuminating during normal highway driving.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle failing to gain traction and sliding on snowy or slippery roads, with the problem appearing on the first exposure to winter conditions. · 3 fire · 6 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the tire pressure monitoring system warning light remaining illuminated continuously, beginning immediately on the drive home from the dealership and persisting despite attempts to reset it. · 1 fire · 1 crash

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report an inability to maintain traction and the vehicle sliding downhill on snowy surfaces, with the problem presenting at the first encounter with winter driving conditions. · 2 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Tires
issues.blurb: Owners report the tire sensor warning indicator illuminating while driving at highway speeds, even after checking that all four tires appear to be properly inflated.
